feed.title: TradeSight Blog — Python Trading Systems
feed.subtitle: Open-source Python algorithmic trading: signals, strategies, and real paper trading results.

entry.title: Building a Python Stock Scanner with RSI Signals
entry.summary: How I built a stock scanner using RSI, MACD, and relative volume signals in Python — with yfinance, pandas, and a circuit breaker that eliminated drawdown spirals. Includes live paper trading results (+6.43% return over 3 weeks on a $532 virtual portfolio).
entry.content:
I've been building TradeSight — an open-source algorithmic trading system written in Python. At its core is a stock scanner that uses momentum signals (RSI, MACD, volume) to surface trade setups across 50+ symbols in real time.

Three weeks of paper trading: +6.43% return on a $532 virtual portfolio. 3 active strategies. 0 crashes since adding a circuit breaker.

This post covers the RSI calculation (the ewm vs rolling gotcha), the multi-signal scoring system, and the simple circuit breaker pattern that stopped the drawdown spiral.

Read the full post →
